meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve can't validate ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't validate ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Design with AI: Validate Ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop guessing and start building. Learn to validate your SaaS ideas using AI-driven design workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code. A student-focused guide.
content.answer_block: Stop building in a vacuum. Use AI-driven rapid prototyping to validate product-market fit before writing production code. By leveraging Antigravity for structural modeling and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can transform abstract concepts into testable MVP interfaces, ensuring your development time is spent on features users actually demand.
content.intro:
You have the vision, the drive, and the sleepless nights, but you’re staring at a blank repository, paralyzed by the fear that nobody actually wants what you’re about to build. This is the 'Founder’s Paradox': you need a product to validate your idea, but you don't want to waste months on an idea that isn't ready. 

It’s time to stop treating code as the first step. By shifting your workflow to AI-assisted design, you turn the validation process into a rapid-fire experiment. We are going to bypass the guesswork and use modern engineering tools to prove your concept while you’re still in the prototype phase.

steps.title: Define the Core Value Proposition
steps.description: Before touching any UI, condense your idea into a single, high-impact problem statement using Antigravity to model the user journey.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project idea: [Insert Idea]. Break it down into a 3-step user journey and identify the core 'Aha!' moment for the user.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Draft a 100-word value proposition and a 3-step user flow document for my idea.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Validate the feasibility of your backend requirements by generating a structured schema that supports your MVP features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a minimalist database schema for a SaaS app that solves [Problem]. Focus on entities required for the core user flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a Prisma schema file based on the user flow identified in step 1.'

steps.title: Rapid UI Prototyping
steps.description: Use AI to convert your schema into visual component structures, ensuring the design aligns with your validated user journey.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a component hierarchy for a React dashboard that implements the core flow defined previously. Use Tailwind CSS conventions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create component files for the dashboard and authentication flow using Tailwind CSS.'

steps.title: Simulate User Interactions
steps.description: Script AI agents to 'test' your components, identifying friction points in your design before you write a single line of business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test suite using Playwright to simulate a user completing the primary task. Identify three potential 'UX dead-ends'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate Playwright test files to simulate the critical path and identify potential UI friction.'

steps.title: Iterate Based on Feedback Loops
steps.description: Use Claude Code to refactor your components based on the identified friction points, tightening the loop between design and implementation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current UI code to address these identified friction points: [Insert Feedback]. Optimize for conversion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Refactor dashboard components to improve conversion based on the identified friction points.'

pro_tips: Treat your AI prompts like professional documentation; the more context you provide about your target persona, the more accurate the code.
Always run your UI through an accessibility audit via AI before finalizing, as poor accessibility is often a proxy for bad UX.
Use Claude Code to create 'mock' API endpoints early; this allows you to test the full frontend flow without waiting for backend completion.
Maintain a 'Decision Log' file where you ask the AI to document why specific architectural choices were made during the build.
If the AI suggests a feature that bloats your MVP, ask it to 'prune the scope to the absolute minimum viable feature set'.
content.next_steps: Once your prototype is validated, deploy it to a staging environment and get it into the hands of 5 potential users. Collect qualitative feedback, then return to this workflow to refine your features.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to outpace the competition. Don't let your ideas die in a notebook—turn them into high-performing SaaS products today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
